game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Geometry Dash Geometry Dash is a rhythm-based platformer where players control a geometric icon, tapping the screen to jump, fly, or flip, navigating a series of obstacles and spikes synchronized to music in auto-scrolling levels. The game's core mechanics involve precise timing and adapting to different game modes (e.g., cube, ship, ball) that alter player movement, with death resulting in a restart from the beginning or a last checkpoint in practice mode.
game_info.description: Geometry Dash is a rhythm-based platformer where you control a geometric icon that automatically moves forward. Navigate through obstacles, spikes, and platforms by jumping at the right time. Progress through increasingly difficult sections, switching between different movement modes. The goal is to reach the end of the level without crashing into any obstacles.
game_info.controls:
SPACE/UP ARROW: Jump (in cube mode) or fly up (in ship mode)
ENTER: Start the game from the title screen
ESC: Pause/unpause the game
R: Restart the game when game over
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Testing basic gameplay functionality and responsiveness by using random jump inputs with varying frequency.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Implement sticky keys with random jumps that change timing every few seconds to ensure the game responds properly to inputs.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game should run without errors, responding to jump inputs and eventually hitting obstacles to reach the game over state.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>

<TEST_2>
<test_description>Testing the win condition by implementing a perfect run through the level.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Precisely time jumps based on player position and upcoming obstacles to navigate the entire course without crashing.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The player should successfully complete the level and reach the win state.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>

<TEST_3>
<test_description>Testing the different game modes (cube and ship) and transitions between them.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Focus on correctly handling the different physics and controls in each mode, ensuring smooth transitions.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The player should correctly switch between cube and ship modes with appropriate physics changes for each mode.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>

<TEST_4>
<test_description>Testing collision detection with various obstacle types (spikes, blocks, ceilings).</test_description>
<strategy_description>Navigate through sections with different obstacle types, ensuring all collision detection works properly.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The player should collide properly with all obstacle types, triggering game over when hitting any obstacle.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>

<TEST_5>
<test_description>Testing game restart functionality after game over.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Play until game over, then test that the level resets properly with the player back at the start position.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>After dying, the game should properly reset all game elements and player position when restarted.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
